1.texto_original: Então Satanás se levantou contra Israel, e incitou David a numerar Israel.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Crônicas 21:1

"Então Satanás se levantou contra Israel, e incitou David a numerar Israel."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Crônicas 21:1

Philological Analysis

"Então Satanás se levantou contra Israel, e incitou David a numerar Israel."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Então Satanás se levantou contra Israel, e incitou David a numerar Israel."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

3.texto_original: Então disse Joab: O Senhor acrescente ao seu povo cem vezes tanto como ele é! Porventura, é rei meu senhor, não são teus os servos de meu senhor? Por que requer isto e meu senhor. Por que traria ele culpa sobre Israel?

5.texto_original: E Joab deu a David o resultado da numeração do povo. E era todo o Israel um milhão e cem mil homens que arrancavam da espada; e de Judá quatrocentos e setenta mil homens que arrancavam da espada.

12.texto_original: ou três anos de fome; ou seres por três meses consumido diante de teus adversários, enquanto a espada de teus inimigos te alcance; ou que por três dias a espada do Senhor, isto é, a peste na terra, e o anjo do Senhor façam destruição por todos os termos de Israel. Vê, pois, agora que resposta hei de levar a quem me enviou.

Devotional Study of 1 Crônicas 21:28

"Nesse mesmo tempo, vendo David que o Senhor lhe respondera na eira de Ornan, o jebuseu, ofereceu ali os seus sacrifícios."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
28.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Crônicas 21:28

Philological Analysis

"Nesse mesmo tempo, vendo David que o Senhor lhe respondera na eira de Ornan, o jebuseu, ofereceu ali os seus sacrifícios."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
28.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Nesse mesmo tempo, vendo David que o Senhor lhe respondera na eira de Ornan, o jebuseu, ofereceu ali os seus sacrifícios."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

29.texto_original: Pois o tabernáculo do Senhor que Moisés fizera no deserto, e o altar do holocausto, estavam naquele tempo no alto de Gibeão;

30.texto_original: mas David não podia ir perante ele para consultar a Deus, porque estava atemorizado por causa da espada do anjo do Senhor.
30.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Crônicas 21:30

"mas David não podia ir perante ele para consultar a Deus, porque estava atemorizado por causa da espada do anjo do Senhor."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.
